topic: Coonawarra terra rossa and climate moderation

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Coonawarra vineyards combine terra rossa topsoil over limestone with a season moderated by Southern Ocean influence.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: The soil profile provides drainage and root access to stored water, while climatic moderation slows ripening and limits excessive heat.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Cabernet Sauvignon can ripen with concentrated flavour while retaining freshness and firm structure.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: terra rossa over limestone and a moderated climate jointly support balanced Cabernet ripening.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
